intake.user_request: Validate downstream consumers (RAG embeddings, analysis skills, snapshot tests) against the post-fix scanner output now that wo/rag-index-chunk-converter-impl has merged to main (origin/main = fe6bdc9). Closes the consumer-side validation gap left by WO-POPULATE-SEMANTIC-RELATIONSHIPS-HEADERS-001 which scoped acceptance to scanner output correctness only.

classification.plan_type_rationale: Multi-task post-merge validation: full test suite re-run, RAG re-index against new output, three analysis-skill spot-checks, snapshot regen. Hard stop after RAG re-index (data-changing operation) and after final review. Not 'strict' (every-phase hard stop is unnecessary overhead for read-only validation tasks). Not 'light' (multiple distinct tasks across different surfaces). 'Phased' fits.

discovery.summary_text: Discovery completed inline during the prior issue-investigator deep-dive on the scanner/output/analysis chain. Key findings: 26 vitests covered producer-side correctness only; consumer-side tests (drift-generator, RAG indexing pipeline, coverage-generator) were not specifically named in the focused run; RAG embeddings on disk pre-date the populate fix and are now stale relative to scanner output; analysis-tool source code did not change on the merged branch (only data files); type contract for ElementData.imports/usedBy is unchanged.

in_scope: Run full vitest suite on origin/main (commit fe6bdc9): npm test (not focused, not --grep)
Capture pre-validation pass/fail baseline; expect 1274 pass / 66 fail (matches post-merge state)
Re-index RAG vectors against the post-fix scanner output via /coderef-rag-server index <project> or rag-index CLI
Confirm new vector index file timestamp post-merge
Spot-check analyze-coderef-semantics: run the skill against the project, verify it parses the newly-written semantic headers without throwing
Spot-check coderef-analyze: run the top-level wrapper, verify it produces an aggregate report
Spot-check data-source-report-use-case-analyzer: run against the new semantic-registry.json, verify it consumes the populated relationship fields
Identify any committed snapshot files (e.g., __tests__/__snapshots__/*.snap) that pin index.json or semantic-registry.json contents; regenerate via vitest --update-snapshot if drift is intentional

Sample one element with rich relationships from .coderef/index.json to confirm the merged fix is producing real data: python -c 'import json; d=json.load(open(".coderef/index.json")); e=[x for x in d["elements"] if x.get("imports") and x.get("usedBy")][0]; print(json.dumps(e, indent=2))'
out_of_scope: 3 pre-existing invalid UUID anchors in foundation-docs gate (warnings only, predate this work — separate stub if pursued)
ASSISTANT tracking-validator pre-existing schema-drift FAIL (predates this work, separate concern)
The 66 pre-existing test failures inherited from main (not regressions from this merge — separate triage)
Any code changes to scanner, generators, or analysis tools (read-only validation only; bug-fix workorders authored separately if regressions surface)
OPENCLAW → automation rename workorder in ASSISTANT (separate concern, addressed independently)

constraints: Read-only validation: this workorder verifies behavior, does not change source code in coderef-core, scanner, or analysis tools
If a regression IS surfaced during validation, halt and author a separate fix workorder rather than patching inline
Snapshot regen is the only file-modifying step; perform it ONLY after the user confirms the diffs are intentional
Do not touch RAG vector data on disk before confirming the running RAG server (port 52849) status; if it died during this session, restart it before re-indexing
